next_pass.focus: Koide formula derivation from (2,1) topological weight + 3 generations

open_gaps: Koide formula derivation: Can we derive Q_leptons=2/3 from (2,1) topological weight + 3 generations + propagation axioms?
φ in particle masses: Is electron→up quark mass ratio ≈ φ³ statistically significant or coincidence? (Monte Carlo test needed)
Full Finsler-Dirac metamaterial design: Detailed engineering proposal specifying metamaterial parameters to match Finsler-Dirac equation exactly

key_findings: Transformation optics (Pendry, Leonhardt 2006) provides rigorous framework for controlling EM fields via coordinate transformations
Analogue gravity field established (Unruh 1981, Barceló 2011 review) -- acoustic, optical, BEC systems model gravitational phenomena
Gordon metric (1923) was first optical spacetime analogue -- predates acoustic black holes
Coulomb force derivable from variational principle (standard electrodynamics)
Hyperbolic metamaterials exhibit 2+1D Lorentz symmetry -- effective gravitational force between photons
Direct derivation of Coulomb force AS refraction (not analogous) NOT found in literature
Key limitation: full GR equivalence requires ε ∝ μ, not generally true in nature

passes.focus: mathematical derivation -- explicit mapping from Coulomb force to Snell's law
key_findings: Direct derivation found: n = sqrt(2m(E-qφ)) + qA·s (from Maupertuis principle)
Randers-Finsler geometry provides the rigorous geometrization of EM force as refraction
Geodesics in a Randers space (F = sqrt(α) + β) are identical to paths of charged particles in EM + Gravity
Fermat's principle for particles: stationary path length in Finsler metric defines the trajectory
Experimental support found: Genov et al. (2009) built Keplerian metamaterials where light orbits exactly like a Coulomb particle
Optical Aharonov-Bohm effect: topological defects in metamaterials create gauge-field phase shifts (Leonhardt)
Ontological identity: forces ARE gradients of effective refractive indices in a Finslerian manifold

passes.focus: Bridging Quantum Mechanics, General Relativity, and QED
key_findings: Spinors and the Dirac equation can be formulated in Finsler geometry over the unit tangent bundle, exhibiting directional dependence
Finsler gravity (Pfeifer & Wohlfarth 2011) generalizes GR field equations; the vacuum is satisfied by a vanishing Finslerian Ricci scalar Ric(x, y) = 0
QED vacuum polarization (Euler-Heisenberg) makes the vacuum a nonlinear, polarizable medium with a field-dependent refractive index n
Axiom 2 (Causal Velocity) maps to the Finslerian null cone; Axiom 3 (Coherence) maps to the stability of the propagation manifold
Vacuum birefringence (n_parallel != n_perp) is the QED manifestation of the Finslerian spin-splitting effect

passes.focus: Mass as Phase Lock and Cosmological Constant as Vacuum Index and Experimental Proposal
key_findings: de Broglie's soliton theory (1924-1927) proposed mass = frequency (m = hν/c²) -- particles are singularities in wave field
Budiyono (2005) showed mass emerges from resonance conditions on soliton internal vibration -- quantization from boundary conditions
Padmanabhan (2014) derived Λ as integration constant determined by CosMIn = 4π -- yields ΛL_P² ≈ 10⁻¹²² exactly
ATLAS 8.2σ confirmation of light-by-light scattering confirms Euler-Heisenberg QED framework
IXPE magnetar observations confirm vacuum birefringence (Π = 15-80% at B = 3-45 B_cr)
Photonic spin Hall effect (Kapitanova 2014, Takayama 2018) demonstrates spin-splitting in hyperbolic metamaterials -- table-top Finsler-Dirac analogue
>27 dB power ratio, λ/15 confinement, works RF to optical frequencies
